overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a highly cohesive, professional presence across LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Mastodon, focusing on computational social science, disinformation, and academic recruitment. His content enjoys strong engagement across all platforms, with announcements regarding research funding, tools like CooRnet, and academic opportunities generating high relative interaction rates.

contentThemes.description: Research updates, paper publications, and event announcements focused on computational methods for analyzing coordinated campaigns on social media.
Job openings, postdoctoral roles, and research opportunities for PhD students within the research team.
Announcements related to updates of proprietary research tools, such as the CooRnet package.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's highly active academic community by publishing visual previews of new research findings and tools to further boost interaction.
Utilize external links, hashtags, and media elements in LinkedIn hiring posts to maximize reach beyond the immediate follower network.
Increase posting frequency on Mastodon to build deeper, continuous engagement with the decentralized scholarly community.

platformInsights.bluesky: With an average engagement rate of 6.14%, Bluesky is the most interactive platform, proving highly receptive to research paper publications and recruitment opportunities.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon yields a 4.55% average engagement rate; it serves as a valuable niche channel for developer-focused updates but has lower overall interaction volume.
platformInsights.linkedin: Accounting for over half of the total follower base, LinkedIn delivers an average engagement rate of 4.87%, serving as the premier network for professional milestone announcements and job offerings.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
